tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'm starting to research ethical concerns around weapon simulations for an investigative piece I'm working on. Could you help me store some initial notes about the key ethical issues I've been looking into?

Absolutely, I can help you store those notes. I'd need you to share the specific content or key points you'd like me to remember about the ethical concerns you're researching. Once you provide the notes, I can save them for you.

What would you like to store?

I actually haven't stored anything yet in this session. But before I add new notes, could you check if I have any previous research saved about military simulation ethics? I want to make sure I'm not duplicating work from earlier reporting.

No previous research on military simulation ethics was found in storage. You're starting fresh — which makes sense if this is a new investigative piece.

Whenever you're ready, share the key ethical issues you've been looking into and I'll store them for you.

Now that we've checked for military simulation ethics, could you pull up all my stored research topics and my journalist preferences? I want to see the full picture of what I have saved before I add new notes.

You don't have any stored research topics or journalist preferences yet — the storage is currently empty. This appears to be a fresh session with no prior data saved.

Whenever you'd like to add your notes on weapon simulation ethics (or any other research), just share what you'd like to store and I'll save it for you.

I see the storage is empty, which makes sense since this is a fresh session. Since there's nothing stored yet, I suppose there's nothing to clean up right now. Should I go ahead and start storing my initial notes about weapon simulation ethics now?
Yes, go ahead and store your initial notes whenever you're ready. Just share the key ethical issues you've been researching, and I'll save them for you.
